learning_objectives: Differentiate cluster A, B, and C personality disorders.
Implement appropriate interventions for borderline and antisocial personality disorders.
Recognize and manage safety risks associated with personality disorders.
key_focus_areas: Risk management for self-harm and violence.

sections.title: Introduction and Scope
sections.content: Personality disorders are enduring patterns of behavior and perception that interfere with daily life. This overview explains why understanding clusters helps nurses plan care.

sections.title: Personality Disorder Clusters
sections.content: Cluster A includes paranoid, schizoid, and schizotypal traits. Cluster B covers antisocial, borderline, histrionic, and narcissistic types. Cluster C encompasses avoidant, dependent, and obsessive‑compulsive personalities.

sections.title: Etiology and Risk Factors
sections.content: Genetics, early trauma, and invalidating environments can disrupt healthy personality development. Cultural factors and brain differences also play a role.

sections.title: Cluster A Features
sections.content: These disorders present with odd or eccentric behaviors. Nursing care focuses on clear communication and gradual trust‑building.

sections.title: Cluster B Features
sections.content: Dramatic and impulsive behaviors dominate this cluster. Borderline and antisocial disorders require firm boundaries and consistent limit setting.

sections.title: Cluster C Features
sections.content: Anxious and fearful patterns drive avoidant, dependent, and obsessive‑compulsive personalities. Nurses encourage coping skills and confidence.

sections.title: Assessment and Diagnosis
sections.content: Look for chronic interpersonal difficulties, inflexible thinking, and maladaptive coping. Screening tools and thorough histories help differentiate disorders.

sections.title: Safety and Crisis Management
sections.content: Self‑harm, aggression, or manipulation may arise. Prioritize safety, use de‑escalation techniques, and collaborate with the care team.

sections.title: Long‑Term Treatment Strategies
sections.content: Therapy, medication for co‑existing symptoms, and skill building improve function. Family education and support groups also promote recovery.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: Understanding personality disorders helps nurses provide consistent care, set boundaries, and foster healthier relationships.
